data.description:
## Context

PLACEHOLDER / FOLLOW-ON epic — not yet designed in detail. Captures the **T2 semantic-resolution tier**
deferred out of the code-grounding v1 floor (epic `8f6c-6008-33f3-4f82`). v1 ships only the deterministic,
polyglot, fail-open **T0 (manifest/registry existence) + T1 (tree-sitter/ast-grep syntactic)** floor and a
**T2 abstain-by-default seam** (a defined `resolver` interface that always returns `unknown` until a backend
is wired). This epic fills that seam with one or more **self-contained** semantic backends.

Why T2 was deferred (the constraints any backend must respect):
- **Not deterministic by nature.** Semantic resolution (does this import resolve / does `.bar` exist on this
  type / does the arity match?) is a function of *machine state* — installed deps, build state, a
  background-indexing language server, language-runtime versions — not just the artifact under review. The same
  code resolves on one setup and fails on another. Every failure mode (not-installed / not-built / not-indexed /
  wrong-version / server-hung) is indistinguishable from "the symbol is genuinely fake."
- **Confirm-only verdict asymmetry.** A T2 `present` is trustworthy (it located a real definition); a T2
  `absent` is *suspect* unless the environment is known-good. So T2 must emit a trustworthy `present` and
  otherwise abstain (`absent` → `unknown`) — it can never sit on a deterministic blocking path.
- **Cannot be borrowed from the ambient environment.** rebar ships to a broad, diverse client base; an
  externally-configured MCP server (e.g. Serena) or a host-installed toolchain is NOT guaranteed where rebar
  runs (CLI, library, a client's `rebar-mcp`). Any T2 backend must be self-contained and opt-in.

## Candidate backends (to be designed)

1. **LSP-client library (bundled).** Take `solidlsp`/`multilspy`-style LSP-client as an optional library
   dependency rebar manages itself (NOT the ambient Serena MCP server). Allowlist of ~12–60 languages, each
   needing its server + runtime prereqs + project config; fail-soft (empty → abstain). Prior art: Monitor-Guided
   Decoding (NeurIPS 2023) and Type-Constrained Code Generation (PLDI 2025) prove the LSP/type-checker is the
   authoritative symbol oracle.
2. **One-shot compiler/checker-CLI diagnostics.** Run `pyright --outputjson` / `tsc --noEmit` / `go build` /
   `cargo check`, parse structured diagnostics (unresolved-import / unknown-attribute / wrong-arity). Batch,
   no long-lived server, simpler to fail-open. The de-facto "diagnostics-in-the-loop" review convention.
3. **Containerized / hermetic resolution (the high-determinism path).** Build the project's *declared dev/build
   environment* and run the resolver inside it — exactly CodeQL's mandated model ("run CodeQL inside the
   container in which you build your code") and Sourcegraph auto-indexing. This makes the machine state a
   declared, versioned input, so resolution becomes reproducible *given the repo* — enough that T2 `absent`
   can graduate from suspect to trustworthy. Design notes:
   - **Target the declared dev/build env, NOT a production Dockerfile** (multi-stage/distroless images strip
     source, compilers, and dev deps). Priority: `devcontainer.json` → a compose dev service → a named build
     stage (`docker build --target builder`) → a Nix dev shell. Never the final prod image.
   - **Determinism ranks with pinning:** Nix flake (hermetic, `flake.lock`, bit-for-bit) > digest-pinned
     container > floating-tag `FROM x:tag` (drifts; not deterministic).
   - **Security is the gating cost:** building untrusted client repos executes arbitrary code (Dockerfile
     `RUN`, devcontainer `postCreateCommand`) — RCE + credential-exfil (cf. the Codespaces token-theft attack).
     Bar = ephemeral single-tenant microVMs + locked egress + no secrets in the build env.
   - Even so it **abstains a lot** (many repos have no usable declared env, or one that won't build from a clean
     clone) — trades result-nondeterminism for availability-nondeterminism, which is why it lives behind the
     abstain-by-default seam rather than replacing it.

## Related (possibly its own sibling epic): IaC grounding lane

Terraform is NOT a code-build environment (it declares cloud infrastructure, won't install language deps or
provide a language server) — so it is out of scope as a "build env for code resolution." But IaC findings have
their *own* tiered grounding that mirrors this model in a different domain: `terraform validate` (offline,
no-cloud reference resolution = IaC T1) + Terraform Registry provider/module existence (= IaC T0) +
`terraform plan` (live, needs creds = IaC T2). The general lesson: **the T0/T1/T2 tiering recurs per domain**;
IaC should be a distinct grounding lane, plausibly split into its own follow-on rather than folded here.

## Success Criteria

- At least one self-contained, opt-in T2 backend implements the v1 `resolver` seam without changing the
  oracle's three-valued evidence contract or its consumers.
- T2 emits a trustworthy `present` and abstains (`unknown`) otherwise; `absent` is only emitted under
  explicit, recorded preconditions (e.g. a successfully-built declared environment).
- T2 is never on a deterministic blocking path and is never sourced from the ambient environment.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] PLACEHOLDER — full design pending; this epic is captured to hold provenance and scope, not yet decomposed.
- [ ] Select the v1 T2 backend(s) among: bundled LSP-client library, one-shot compiler-CLI diagnostics,
      containerized/hermetic (devcontainer/Nix) resolution — with the cost/security/determinism tradeoffs decided.
- [ ] Backend(s) implement the existing T2 seam from epic `8f6c` behind the unchanged three-valued evidence
      contract (outcome + reason + provenance tier); consumers require no change.
- [ ] Confirm-only verdict policy enforced: trustworthy `present`; `absent` only under recorded preconditions;
      all flaky/missing/timeout/unsupported cases → `unknown` with a structured reason.
- [ ] Self-contained + opt-in: no reliance on any ambient MCP server or host toolchain; gated behind an
      optional extra; abstains cleanly when its prerequisites are absent.
- [ ] (If containerized) executes only the *declared dev/build* artifact (devcontainer/compose-dev/named build
      stage/Nix shell), never a production image; runs in an isolated, egress-locked, secret-free sandbox.
- [ ] Decide whether the IaC grounding lane (`terraform validate` + Registry existence + `plan`) is in scope
      here or split into its own sibling epic.
- [ ] Fail-open tested per backend (unsupported lang / no server / no buildable env / crash / timeout → unknown).
data.title: Code-grounding T2: self-contained semantic-resolution backends behind the abstain-by-default seam (LSP-lib / compiler-CLI / containerized)
